The primary treatment for urea cycle disorders involves reducing ammonia levels, which can be achieved through various drugs. One commonly prescribed drug is phenylbutyrate (option B), as it helps convert glutamine into amino acids, thereby decreasing ammonia production. Other options like carnitine or branched-chain amino acids may support overall metabolism but aren't primarily used for directly managing high ammonia levels.

Answer: B
